issue_title: ESQL: zero_terms_query is not accepted for MATCH even though it should be accepted
issue_body:
### Description

```
from employees
| where match(first_name, "foo", {"zero_terms_query": "all"})
```

results in 

```
"type": "illegal_argument_exception",
"reason": "illegal match option [zero_terms_query]",
"stack_trace": "org.elasticsearch.ElasticsearchException$1: illegal match option [zero_terms_query]
```

While writing 

```
from employees
| where match(first_name, "foo", {"foobar": "all"})
```

will have an error that mention `zero_terms_query` as being accepted:

```
"reason": "Found 1 problem\nline 3:9: Invalid option [foobar] in [match(first_name, \"foo\", {\"foobar\": \"all\"})], expected one of [auto_generate_synonyms_phrase_query, minimum_should_match, prefix_length, lenient, zero_terms_query, analyzer, fuzzy_transpositions, fuzzy_rewrite, fuzziness, max_expansions, boost, operator]",
```

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix Driver search load millis as nanos bug (#143267)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/143111
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/143086
Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/142986

Introduced in https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/142841

The `now` variable holds millis, while the `reportSearchLoad()` expects nanos.
Propagated `nowSupplier` there instead to pass the actual nanos.

issue_title: ESQL: multi_match with a lookup index fails with AssertionError
issue_body:
### Description

```
from languages*
| keep language_code_long, language_code
| mv_expand language_code 
| where NOT multi_match("test", language_code_long)
```

results in


issue_body:
### Description

```
row x = null | uri_parts y = x
```

results in 

```
Input for URI_PARTS must be of type [string] but is [null]"
```

fyi @alex-spies
commit_message:
ESQL: Allow `null`s in commands (#143195)

* ESQL: Allow `null`s in commands

Allows `null` *typed* columns in:
* `DISSECT`
* `GROK`
* `REGISTER_DOMAIN`
* `URI_PARTS`

These always make `null` valued results.

Closes #143145
Closes #142860

issue_title: ES|QL with large QueryDSL filters can OOM
issue_body: We've seen an issue recently with a user that had an ES|QL query that includes a QueryDSL filter that contains a `geo_shape` filter doing a `within` check on a very large polygon (eg. the border of an entire country). This results in `OperatorStatus.processedQueries` map containing very large keys. The heap dump analysis showed a path to the memory that looked like: * compute.operator.OperatorStatus * compute.lucene.LuceneOperator$Status * processedQueries * TreeMap * root * key We should investigate if the processedQueries needs to maintain the full polygon information here, or if this could be replaced by a hashcode instead. Also, check that this is protected by a circuit-breaker.

commit_message:
Fix int overflow in vector similarity bounds checks for segments larger than 2GB (#143241)

The bulk bounds check methods in JdkVectorLibrary cast MemorySegment.byteSize() to int when validating the dataset segment size. When the segment exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE (~2 GB), this overflows to a negative value, causing a spurious IndexOutOfBoundsException.

We can fix this by using the Objects.checkFromIndexSize(long, long, long) overload for the dataset segment checks, which avoids the narrowing cast entirely.

closes #143232

commit_message:
Fix and unmute HeapAttack test (#143612)

Previously with the legacy LookupFromIndexOperator this test would throw CircuitBreakerException fast.
With the new StreamingLookupFromIndexOperator this test will not throw CircuitBreakerException any more. However, as the join is expanding, we are joining on 30 columns and data to join is large, sometimes it will timeout. The fix is to reduce the amount of data to join, so it does not timeout.

Closes #143347

commit_message:
Make large segment vector tests resilient to memory constraints (#143366)

The test in JDKVectorLibraryLargeSegmentTests failswith OutOfMemoryError when the maximum memory size is insufficient for the ~2GB Arena.allocate() call. The solution is to wrap the allocation in a tryAllocate helper that catches OutOfMemoryError and skips the test gracefully via assumeTrue instead of failing.

closes: #143356 #143355 #143354 #143353

commit_message:
Fix `ConcurrentSnapshotsIT#testBackToBackQueuedDeletes` (#144461)

This test fails if it only has a single snapshot thread and that thread
gets blocked on the repository before the second snapshot has been
enqueued: since #141998 landed, enqueuing batches of deletes requires a
snapshot thread. This commit fixes the problem by adding a second
snapshot thread.

Closes #143387

issue_title: ESQL: unmapped_fields nullify/load: double check interaction with PromQL queries
issue_body:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/141532 kind of dictates how `SET unmapped_fields="..."` interacts with `PROMQL` queries: the setting has no effect and missing/unmapped fields are always nullified.

Let's add a couple of tests with PROMQL to put that behavior into code.

There's also a question if `SET unmapped_fields="nullify"` and `...="load"` should raise a warning or cause the query to fail given that it currently silently doesn't have any effect on it. In particular, `load` will never actually load anything from `_source` for such queries.

Filing as bug for now, given that we may currently have the wrong behavior if we decide that `PROMQL` queries with `load` should fail.
commit_message: [ES|QL] Reject PromQL queries when unmapped_fields=load (#144791)

issue_title: ESQL: TS command ignores aliases in BY
issue_body:
Reproduces with the csv data set:
```
TS k8s | STATS max_bytes=max(to_long(network.total_bytes_in)) BY foobar = cluster

   max_bytes   |    cluster    
---------------+---------------
10797          |qa             
7403           |staging        
10277          |prod  
```
The aliasing of `cluster` to `foobar` is being ignored. The second column should be called `foobar`.
```
TS k8s | STATS max_bytes=max(to_long(network.total_bytes_in)) BY foobar = cluster | keep max_bytes, foobar

   max_bytes   |    cluster    
---------------+---------------
10797          |qa             
7403           |staging        
10277          |prod  

{"error":{"root_cause":[{"type":"illegal_state_exception","reason":"Found 1 problem\nline 1:1: Plan [Project[[max_bytes{r}#397, foobar{r}#394]]] optimized incorrectly due to missing references [foobar{r}#394]"}],"type":"illegal_state_exception","reason":"Found 1 problem\nline 1:1: Plan [Project[[max_bytes{r}#397, foobar{r}#394]]] optimized incorrectly due to missing references [foobar{r}#394]"},"status":500}
```

commit_message:
Fix AsyncSearchIndexServiceTests.testCircuitBreaker failure (#144058)

Closes #143411.

The circuit breaker test did not take into account the overhead in ByteArrayWrapper when picking a size of the circuit breaker to be large enough to accommodate the page and ByteArrayWrapper. With an unlucky random seed, the test would fail immediately for large enough pages.

commit_message:
Fix flaky MMR diversification YAML tests (#143706)

The default int8_hnsw index type quantizes float32 vectors to
int8, introducing enough scoring error to non-deterministically
reorder documents with close cosine similarities. With only 4
dimensions the quantization is particularly coarse.

Use explicit hnsw index type on test dense_vector mappings to
get exact float scoring and deterministic KNN result ordering.
Update expected results to reflect exact cosine ordering.

Closes #143430
Closes #143609

commit_message:
Fix IndexInputUtils.withSlice to produce native-safe MemorySegments on Java 21 (#143479)

On Java 21, FFI disallows passing heap-backed MemorySegments to native downcalls. Java 22+ removes this restriction.

IndexInputUtils.withSlice is now the single path through which all simdvec scorers obtain MemorySegments from IndexInput data, and several of those scorers pass the segment directly to native downcalls. Rather than patching each and every call site across scorer classes individually, this fix takes a "safety by design" approach: withSlice itself now guarantees that the segment it hands to callers is always native-safe, regardless of Java version. No current or future caller needs to worry about the heap-segment restriction, correctness is enforced at the source.

I also added an assertion to the DirectAccessInput path that the byte buffer is direct, documenting the invariant that real implementations always provide native-backed buffers.

The tradeoff here is that on Java 21 only, the copyAndApply fallback path incurs an extra native allocation and copy (via a confined Arena) where a heap-backed segment would have sufficed, since a number of call sites only use the Panama Vector API and never touch native downcalls. On Java 22+ the behavior is unchanged. This is an acceptable cost: the fallback path is already the slowest path (mmap and direct buffer paths are preferred), and the alternative - requiring every call site to independently guard against heap segments, is fragile and has already proven easy to miss. We can do some further cleanup at the call site after this fix has been merged.

caused by #141718
closes #143441

issue_title: `_timeseries` metadata field on downsampled indices includes `_doc_count`
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

main

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

N/A

### Problem Description

When executing the following ES|QL query on downsampled index:

```
TS my-metrics | STATS rate=RATE(counter) BY time_bucket = TBUCKET(1 hour) | SORT time_bucket
```

we receive back 3 columns, `rate, _timeseries, time_bucket`. 

We expect the `_timeseries` field to contain all the dimensions. However, we observed that when the document has the `_doc_count` set, then this is also included in the `_timeseries` column. 

This bug seems to be only a display bug and it does not actually consider the `_doc_count` as a dimension.
commit_message:
Apply source filter to metadata field mappers when loading synthetic source (#143726)

When loading synthetic fields we use the `SourceFilter` to determine which ones will be included. However, `ObjectMapper` includes all the metadata fields as well. 

Usually, this is not an issue because most metadata fields do not support synthetic source. 

However, the `_doc_count` does and when we were displaying the dimensions in ES|QL of a downsampled index where all docs have the `_doc_count` set, it adds `_doc_count` in the output of `_timeseries`.

In this PR, we change the `innerSyntheticFieldLoader` of the `ObjectMapper` to apply the filter also on the metadata. We do always include the `IgnoredSourceFieldMapper` because we need it to load the fields from it.

Closes #143464

issue_title: ESQL: unmapped_fields="load" should not try to load subfields of flattened fields
issue_body:
Flattened fields aren't supported, yet, in ESQL - but we know when a field is of the flattened type (same for object).

If `foo` is a flattened field, then `foo.bar` is not an unrelated field - it's clearly a subfield of the flattened field.

`SET unmapped_field="load"` will treat `foo.bar` as unrelated and load it from source.

That is generally desirable behavior, but we don't know, yet, if the semantics for subfields of flattened fields will be fully consistent with semantics of unmapped fields. The consequence is that queries using both `load` and subfields of flattened (or object) fields may break once we release proper support for flattened (/object) fields.

Let's disallow or disable loading via `load` for flattened and object fields for now.

To be more precise: the following works in current SNAPSHOT, but shouldn't:
```
curl -u elastic:password -H "Content-Type: application/json" "127.0.0.1:9200/test3" -XPUT -d '{
  "mappings": { "dynamic": false,
    "properties": { "foo": {"type": "flattened"}
    }
  }
}'

curl -u elastic:password -HContent-Type:application/json 'localhost:9200/test3/_doc?refresh' -d'{"foo": {"bar": "baz"}}'  

curl -u elastic:password -H "Content-Type: application/json" "127.0.0.1:9200/_query?format=txt" -d '
{
  "query": "SET unmapped_fields=\"load\"; FROM test3 METADATA _source | keep foo.bar"}'
    foo.bar    
---------------
baz  
```
commit_message:
[ES|QL] unmapped_fields="load" should not try to load subfields of flattened fields (#144190)

- Expand field caps request, so that for queries referencing "foo.bar", it also retrieves mapping for "foo", in addition to "foo.bar" and "foo.bar.*". Only done when unmapped_fields="load".
- Prevent loading subfields of flattened fields from the verifier
- Add yaml tests, unit tests, and capability.
- Temporarily ignore csv tests that load subfields of flattened field while setting unmapped_fields="load".

issue_title: Scroll responds with 503 when no shards match
commit_message:
Return an empty response for scrolls with no shard contexts (#148509)

When the initial _search?scroll=… resolves to zero shards, either because the index expression matched no indices, or because every shard was filtered out by can_match, the coordinator still returns a _scroll_id. Calling POST /_search/scroll with that id used to fail with 503 SearchPhaseExecutionException: no nodes to search on (see reproducers in #143532).

The fix short-circuits in TransportSearchScrollAction.doExecute right after parsing the scroll id. When scrollId.getContext().length == 0 we skip the async-action machinery entirely and respond with an empty 200. The scroll id is returned so a client can run its usual iteration without having to special-case the error.

As part of this PR the existing SearchResponse.empty(Supplier<Long>, Clusters) factory has been replaced with a small EmptyResponseBuilder.

On the test side, there's a YAML test for each of the two scenarios in the issue (both gated on a new search.scroll.empty_context_returns_200 cluster feature for BWC), a SearchScrollIT integration test for the empty-pattern case, and unit tests for emptyResponseBuilder() covering the response shape and defaults.

Closes #143532

# Reference

## Publish your docs

[`docs/reference/query-languages/toc.yml`](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/main/docs/reference/query-languages/toc.yml) is the source of truth for what gets published. A page that exists on disk but isn't listed there won't appear in the navigation or be published.

### Hide a page before it's ready

To merge docs before a feature is publicly available, use `hidden` instead of `file` in `toc.yml`:

```yaml
- hidden: commands/my-command.md
```

Also comment out the corresponding entry in [`_snippets/lists/`](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/tree/main/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/_snippets/lists) with a `%` prefix:

```markdown
% * [My Command](../commands/my-command.md)
```

> [!NOTE]
> The page stays accessible via direct URL but won't appear in navigation or search. You don't need to change any `.md` content files. See [#142859](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/142859/changes) for an example.
